calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-05-2021 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Selene Ballonoff; 510-549-3709; selenebnl@gmail.com; Plaintiff in propria persona.
Vincent K. Yu, Esq. (SBN 295578) (415) 777-3300; law@solanpark.com; for Defendant Tom Lucas

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANT THOMAS LUCAS' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Defendant's unopposed request for judicial notice of related pleadings and orders is granted. The Court declines to consider plaintiff's late-filed opposition. (See Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1005(b); Cal. R. Ct. 3.1300(d).) In any event, plaintiff has already brought this case and lost it. As she concedes, her claims against the conservator have been the subject of extensive litigation in conservatorship proceedings including a trial, and are now pending on appeal. (See Def. RJN, Ex. C (Oct. 27, 2020 memorandum order, order approving acts of conservator, and order denying petition to limit authority of conservator); Ex. G (Jan. 4, 2021 order denying motion for new trial.) She also has been declared a vexatious litigant, at least in part as a result of the suits she has filed against defendant. Even if the instant action is properly brought in the Civil Division rather than in the Probate and Mental Health Division of the Court, which is doubtful, plaintiff's attempt to relitigate the same claims in yet another duplicative action is improper.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)
